title: चन्द्रशेखराष्टकम्
devanagariText:
चन्द्रशेखर चन्द्रशेखर चन्द्रशेखर पाहि माम्।
चन्द्रशेखर चन्द्रशेखर चन्द्रशेखर रक्ष माम्॥

रत्नसानुशरासनं रजतादिशृंगनिकेतनं
शिञ्जिनीकृतपन्नगेश्वरमच्युतानलसायकम्।
क्षिप्रदग्धपुरत्रयं त्रिदिवालयैरभिवन्दितं
चन्द्रशेखरमाश्रये मम किं करिष्यति वै यमः॥१॥

पञ्चपादपुष्पगन्धपदाम्बुजद्वयशोभितं
फालदेशविलम्बमानवरालकम्बिविभूतिकम्।
मञ्जुवीणकनूपुरोज्ज्वलनृत्तपादसरोरुहं
चन्द्रशेखरमाश्रये मम किं करिष्यति वै यमः॥२॥

मत्तवारणमुख्यचर्मकृतोत्तरीयमनोहरं
पंकजासनपद्मलोचनपूजिताङ्घ्रिसरोरुहम्।
देवसिन्धुतरंगसीकरसिक्तशुभ्रजटाधरं
चन्द्रशेखरमाश्रये मम किं करिष्यति वै यमः॥३॥

यक्षराजसखं भगाक्षहरं भुजंगविभूषणं
शैलराजसुतापरिष्कृतचारुवामकलेबरम्।
क्ष्वेडनीलगलं परश्वधधारिणं मृगधारिणं
चन्द्रशेखरमाश्रये मम किं करिष्यति वै यमः॥४॥

कुण्डलीकृतकुण्डलेश्वरकुण्डलं वृषवाहनं
नारदादिमुनीश्वरस्तुतवैभवं भुवनेश्वरम्।
आन्ध्रकान्तकमाश्रितामरपादपं शमनान्तकं
चन्द्रशेखरमाश्रये मम किं करिष्यति वै यमः॥५॥

भेषजं भवरोगिणामखिलापदामपहारिणं
दक्षयज्ञविनाशनं त्रिगुणात्मकं त्रिविलोचनम्।
भुक्तिमुक्तिफलप्रदं सकलागमैकलक्षणं
चन्द्रशेखरमाश्रये मम किं करिष्यति वै यमः॥६॥

भक्तवत्सलमर्चितं निधिमक्षयं हरिदम्बरं
सर्वभूतपतिं परात्परमप्रमेयमनुत्तमम्।
सोमवारिदभूहुताशनसोमपानिलखाकृतिं
चन्द्रशेखरमाश्रये मम किं करिष्यति वै यमः॥७॥

विश्वसृष्टिविधायिनं पुनरेव पालनतत्परं
संहरन्तमपि प्रपञ्चमशेषलोकनिवासिनम्।
क्रीडयन्तमहर्निशं गणनाथयूथसमन्वितं
चन्द्रशेखरमाश्रये मम किं करिष्यति वै यमः॥८॥

मृत्युभीतमृकण्डुसूनुकृतस्तवं शिवसन्निधौ
यत्र कुत्र च यः पठेन्न हि तस्य मृत्युभयं भवेत्।
पूर्णमायुररोगितामखिलार्थसम्पदमादरात्
चन्द्रशेखरमेव नित्यमुपासते स हि मोक्षभाक्॥

॥ इति श्रीचन्द्रशेखराष्टकं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
Chandrashekhar Chandrashekhar Chandrashekhar Pahi Maam.
Chandrashekhar Chandrashekhar Chandrashekhar Raksha Maam.

Ratnasanusharasanam Rajatadishringanikatanam
Shinjineekritapannageshvaramachyutanalasayakam.
Kshipradagdhapuratrayam Tridivalayairabhivanditam
Chandrashekharam-Ashraye Mama Kim Karishyati Vai Yamah.||1||

Panchapada-Pushpagandha-Padambujadvaya-Shobhitam
Phaladeshavilambamana-Varalakambi-Vibhootikam.
ManjuveeNaka-Noopurojjvala-Nritta-Pada-Saroruham
Chandrashekharam-Ashraye Mama Kim Karishyati Vai Yamah.||2||

MattavaranamUkhyacharma-Kritottareeya-Manoharam
Pankajaasana-Padmalochana-Poojitanghri-Saroruham.
Devasindhu-Tarangaseekara-Sikta-Shubhrajata-Dharam
Chandrashekharam-Ashraye Mama Kim Karishyati Vai Yamah.||3||

YaksharajaSakham Bhagaakshaharam Bhujangavibhooshanam
Shailarajasutaa-Parishkrita-Chaaruvaamakalebaram.
Kshvedaneelam-Galam Parashvadadharinam Mrigadharinam
Chandrashekharam-Ashraye Mama Kim Karishyati Vai Yamah.||4||

KundaleeKrita-Kundaleshvara-Kundalam Vrishavaahanam
Naaradaadi-Muneeshvara-Stuta-Vaibhavam Bhuvaneshvaram.
Aandhrakaantakamaashritaamarapaadapam Shamanaantakam
Chandrashekharam-Ashraye Mama Kim Karishyati Vai Yamah.||5||

Bheshajam Bhavaroginaamakhilaapadaamapaharinam
DakshayajnaVinaashanam Trigunaatmakam Trivilochanam.
Bhuktimuktiphalapradam Sakalaagamaikaalakshanam
Chandrashekharam-Ashraye Mama Kim Karishyati Vai Yamah.||6||

Bhaktavatsalamarchitam Nidhimakshayam Haridambaram
Sarvabhootapatim Paraatparamaprameyamanuttamam.
SomavaridabhooHutaashana-Somapanilakhakritim
Chandrashekharam-Ashraye Mama Kim Karishyati Vai Yamah.||7||

VishvasrishtiVidhayinam Punareva Palaanatatparam
Samharantamapi Prapanchamasheshalokanivasinam.
Kreedayantam-Aharnisham Gananaathayoothasamanvitam
Chandrashekharam-Ashraye Mama Kim Karishyati Vai Yamah.||8||

MrityubheetaMrikandusoonukritastavam Shivasannidhau
Yatra Kutra Cha Yah Pathenna Hi Tasya Mrityubhayam Bhavet.
Poornamaayurarogiitaamakhilaarthasampadamaadarat
Chandrashekharam-Eva Nityam-Upaasate Sa Hi Mokshabhak.

|| Iti Shri Chandrashekhara-Ashtakam Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
हे चन्द्रशेखर! मेरी रक्षा करो, मुझे बचाओ।

१. जिनका धनुष मेरु पर्वत है, जो रजत शृंग (कैलास) पर निवास करते हैं, जिन्होंने वासुकि नाग को प्रत्यञ्चा बनाया, विष्णु को बाण बनाया, तीन पुरों को क्षणभर में भस्म कर दिया, त्रिलोक के देवों द्वारा वन्दित — ऐसे चन्द्रशेखर की मैं शरण लेता हूँ, यम मेरा क्या करेगा?

२. जिनके दोनों चरणकमल पंचपदी पुष्पों की सुगन्ध से शोभित हैं, ललाट पर लटकती अलकों में विभूति है, मधुर वीणा और नूपुर से उज्ज्वल नृत्य-चरण हैं — ऐसे चन्द्रशेखर की शरण में यम क्या करेगा?

३. मत्त गजचर्म का उत्तरीय धारण करने वाले, ब्रह्मा-विष्णु द्वारा पूजित चरणों वाले, देवनदी गंगा की तरंगों से सिञ्चित शुभ्र जटाधारी — चन्द्रशेखर की शरण में यम क्या करेगा?

४. कुबेर के मित्र, भग की आँखें हरने वाले, सर्प-आभूषण, पार्वती से सुशोभित वामांग, हलाहल से नीलकण्ठ, परशु और मृग धारी — चन्द्रशेखर की शरण में यम क्या करेगा?

५. कुण्डली बने नागराज के कुण्डल वाले, वृषभवाहन, नारदादि मुनियों द्वारा स्तुत, भुवनेश्वर, अन्धकासुर-संहारक, कल्पवृक्ष समान, यमान्तक — चन्द्रशेखर की शरण में यम क्या करेगा?

६. भवरोगियों की औषधि, सब आपदाओं को हरने वाले, दक्ष-यज्ञ-विनाशक, त्रिगुणात्मक, त्रिलोचन, भुक्ति-मुक्ति फलदाता, सकल आगमों के एकमात्र लक्ष्य — चन्द्रशेखर की शरण में यम क्या करेगा?

७. भक्तवत्सल, अक्षय निधि, दिगम्बर, सर्वभूतपति, परात्पर, अप्रमेय, अनुत्तम, पृथ्वी-जल-अग्नि-वायु-आकाश रूप — चन्द्रशेखर की शरण में यम क्या करेगा?

८. विश्व की सृष्टि करने वाले, फिर पालन करने वाले, फिर संहार करने वाले, सर्वलोकनिवासी, दिन-रात गणनाथों के साथ क्रीड़ा करने वाले — चन्द्रशेखर की शरण में यम क्या करेगा?

मृत्यु से भयभीत मार्कण्डेय (मृकण्डुपुत्र) द्वारा रचित यह स्तव जो कहीं भी शिव-सान्निध्य में पढ़े, उसे मृत्युभय नहीं होता। पूर्ण आयु, आरोग्य, सम्पूर्ण अर्थ-सम्पत्ति प्राप्त होती है और जो नित्य चन्द्रशेखर की उपासना करता है वह मोक्ष का भागी होता है।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीचन्द्रशेखराष्टकस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य मार्कण्डेय ऋषिः। स्रग्धरा छन्दः। श्री चन्द्रशेखर (शिव) देवता। ॐ नमः शिवाय बीजम्। मृत्युञ्जय शक्तिः। मृत्युभय निवारण कीलकम्। श्रीचन्द्रशेखरप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Conquers the fear of death — composed by Markandeya who defeated Yama
Invokes long life, health, and freedom from untimely death
Each verse affirms 'What can Yama do to me?' — supreme protection
Invokes the blessing of wealth, prosperity, and fulfillment of all desires
Invokes moksha to the devoted daily reciter
Extremely powerful when recited on Maha Shivaratri
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of types of fears and anxieties
description: Chandrashekhar Ashtakam — चन्द्रशेखराष्टकम् — is an eight-verse devotional hymn offered to Lord Shiva. This revered text originates from Composed by Markandeya Rishi. Regular recitation is believed to grant long life, health, and freedom from untimely death, remove all types of fears and anxieties, and grant moksha to the devoted daily reciter. It is especially recommended on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri and Shravan month for maximum spiritual benefit. With 8 verses and a reading time of about 7 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Chandrashekhar Ashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Chandrashekhar Ashtakam by Markandeya in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Eight verses to conquer death fear and gain Shiva's supreme protection.
relatedSearches: Chandrashekhar Ashtakam Sanskrit lyrics with meaning
Chandrashekhar Ashtakam refrain Chandrashekara mam pahi
Chandrasekhara Ashtakam audio MP3 traditional pandits
Shiva Ashtakam vs Chandrashekhar Ashtakam comparison
Chandrashekhar Ashtakam protection from untimely death

seoFaqs.question: What is the legendary origin of the Chandrashekhar Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ashtakam is traditionally attributed to Sage Markandeya, who composed it spontaneously in the moment of his rescue from death. According to the Markandeya Purana, Markandeya was destined by Brahma's boon to live only sixteen years. As he reached his sixteenth birthday, Yama, the lord of death, came to claim him; Markandeya, a devoted Shiva-bhakta, embraced the Shiva-linga in terror. Yama threw his rope-noose; the noose fell on the linga along with Markandeya, and Shiva burst out of the linga, kicked Yama and rescued the boy, granting him the boon of being chiranjivi (deathless) at sixteen forever. The Chandrashekhar Ashtakam is the eight-verse song of gratitude that Markandeya sang at this moment, addressing Shiva specifically as Chandrashekhara, the bearer of the moon. (Sources: gurukripa.org.in Chandrashekhar Ashtakam; sathyasaiwithstudents.blogspot.com; bhaktinidhi.com; sanatanweb.com.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Shiva specifically addressed as Chandrashekhara in this Ashtakam rather than other names?
seoFaqs.answer: Chandrashekhara literally means he-who-has-the-moon-as-his-crown, referring to the crescent moon that Shiva wears on his matted locks. Mythologically, Shiva placed Chandra on his head when the Moon was withering under Daksha's curse of waning, providing perpetual refuge and granting Chandra the ability to wax again. The name therefore signifies Shiva as the supreme refuge for those facing decay and death; Markandeya, facing the decay of his own life-span, addresses Shiva precisely in this aspect of cosmic refuge-giver. The crescent moon also signifies time itself, marked by the lunar calendar; Shiva as Chandrashekhara is the master of time who can grant relief from the decree of Yama. The refrain Chandrashekhara mam pahi (Chandrashekhara, protect me) repeated in every verse encapsulates this theology. (Sources: gurukripa.org.in; yantrachants.com; isha.sadhguru.org Shiva Ashtakam; shravanmanyam.blogspot.com.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the structural pattern of the eight verses?
seoFaqs.answer: Each of the eight verses follows an identical structural pattern: three padas describing one major aspect or exploit of Shiva (his cosmic destruction, his samhara of demons, his ash-smeared body, his three eyes, his bull-vehicle Nandi, his consort Parvati, his austerities at Kailasa), and the fourth pada the unvarying refrain Chandrashekara Chandrashekara Chandrashekara pahi mam (Chandrashekhara, Chandrashekhara, Chandrashekhara, protect me). This refrain-pattern, similar to that of the Mahimna Stotra, makes the Ashtakam easily memorisable and gives it the rhythmic intensity of a pleading invocation. The accumulation of eight different aspects of Shiva, each followed by the same refuge-cry, has been likened by traditional commentators to a soul circling a single shelter from many directions. (Sources: gurukripa.org.in; sanskritdocuments.org Chandrashekhara; sathyasaiwithstudents.blogspot.com; sanatanweb.com.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Chandrashekhar Ashtakam claim?
seoFaqs.answer: The principal benefit, anchored in Markandeya's own deliverance, is protection from untimely death (akala-mrityu) and from any threat to life including illness, accident, surgery, legal sentence, or assassination. Beyond this primary phala, the Ashtakam is recited for fearlessness, freedom from karmic decay, recovery from chronic and terminal illness, peace at the death-bed (when recited for a dying person), and after death for the smooth passage of the departed soul. It is also used as a Mrityunjaya-substitute when the Mahamrityunjaya Mantra cannot be recited the prescribed number of times; eight verses of Chandrashekhar Ashtakam are held to be equivalent to one round of 108 Mahamrityunjaya japa. The text is therefore foundational to Shaiva life-protection liturgy. (Sources: gurukripa.org.in; sathyasaiwithstudents.blogspot.com; yantrachants.com 6 paths; blog.cosmicinsights.net.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the most powerful day to recite Chandrashekhar Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: Mahashivratri (Phalguna Krishna Chaturdashi, February-March) is the supreme occasion, with the Ashtakam recited at each of the four prahara-pujas through the night. Pradosha (the trayodashi tithi twice each lunar month) is the next-most-powerful occasion; the Ashtakam is the principal Pradosha-text in many Shaiva households. Krishna Chaturdashi (the day before amavasya) is favoured for Mrityunjaya-related recitation. Every Monday (Shiva-day) and the Saumya-vara of Aslesha, Mrigashira and Hasta nakshatras are weekly occasions. For specific health-recovery sankalpas, an unbroken 41-day or 108-day parayana is undertaken, with the Ashtakam recited a fixed number of times daily (commonly 11, 21 or 108) at brahma-muhurta. (Sources: gurukripa.org.in; isha.sadhguru.org; bhaktinidhi.com; blog.cosmicinsights.net.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Chandrashekhar Ashtakam relate to the Mahamrityunjaya Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: Both texts share the same theological purpose (protection from death) and are anchored in Shiva's mastery over time and decay, but they operate at different liturgical scales. The Mahamrityunjaya Mantra (Tryambakam yajamahe) is a single Vedic mantra of two padas drawn from Rig Veda 7.59.12, recited in fixed counts (108, 1008, 12500, 125000 etc.) under strict mantra-shastra rules. The Chandrashekhar Ashtakam is a Puranic stotra of eight verses recited as a complete bhakti unit, with no count restriction. The Ashtakam is more accessible to those without mantra-diksha, while the Mantra is more concentrated and is typically reserved for diksha-holders or for use under priest-led puja. Most Shaiva life-protection programmes combine both: the Mantra for its concentrated mantra-shakti, the Ashtakam for its bhakti-warmth and Markandeya-association. (Sources: gurukripa.org.in; isha.sadhguru.org; yantrachants.com; blog.cosmicinsights.net.)
